title: README documents a --json flag the CLI no longer accepts
description: The README usage block lists `--json` as a supported flag, but the argument parser in cli.py has no such option — invoking it errors with 'unrecognized arguments: --json'.

evidence.snippet: Run `tool report --json` to emit machine-readable output.

suggested_action: Either restore the --json flag in cli.py or remove the stale README line.

title: No __main__ guard in cli.py despite docs calling it a runnable script
description: An absence claim: the docs describe `python cli.py` as runnable, but cli.py defines no `if __name__ == "__main__":` entrypoint. The line_range anchors the representative witness line (the last top-level def) proving the absence.

title: Docs claim secrets come from env vars, but a literal API key is hardcoded
description: A two-sided doc-vs-code contradiction: SECURITY.md states all credentials are injected via environment variables, while config.py assigns a literal API key. The doc side and the code side disagree.
suggested_action: Remove the hardcoded key and read it from the documented environment variable; rotate the exposed credential.
